published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT: boundary-tail EVT normalization analogue, but not for CCOT PRTE endpoints

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest repo entries are stat_ate_overlap_decay and STAT_PolicyRegretMarginOverlap_Research, which concern ATE or policy-regret weak-overlap rates rather than PRTE OT endpoint experiments

published_prior_art.cite: TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives CCOT PRTE bounds and continuous-instrument rates, not a boundary local experiment

TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives rate characterization; FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID gives entropic-regularized OT inference, not the unregularized boundary-local CCOT PRTE endpoint law
CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets supply general adaptation lower-bound logic, but not a CCOT PRTE two-tail construction
ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI are the regular endpoint CI template
none; FangSantos2019DirectionalInference and FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID do not construct fixed-alpha honest CIs for the unregularized CCOT PRTE boundary endpoint

novelty_flags.label: thm:no-honest-adaptation
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship adaptation lower bound cites CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets without theorem, modulus, or page anchors tying their general adaptation logic to the claimed two-tail CCOT construction.

soundness_flags.label: def:boundary-radius / prop:phase-normalization
soundness_flags.one_line: With b_n(P)=inf{t in (0,bar_b] : n t^2 V_P(t) <= 1}, the set contains arbitrarily small t under the stated tail classes, so the infimum is 0 and r_n(P)=1/b_n(P) is not a well-defined finite normalization.

soundness_flags.label: ass:boundary-tail / prop:phase-normalization
soundness_flags.one_line: The two-sided tail comparability assumption c_- t^alpha <= T_P(t) <= c_+ t^alpha supports order bounds for V_P(t), not the asserted asymptotic equivalence to a positive multiple without an added regular-variation or tail-limit condition.

soundness_flags.label: prop:strict-overlap-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Strict overlap, B bounded away from zero, is incompatible with the lower tail-mass condition defining P_n(alpha), so the proposition cannot simply depend on thm:endpoint-limit-law over P_n(alpha) without a separate strict-overlap class or extension theorem.

soundness_flags.label: ass:regular-linearization
soundness_flags.one_line: SasakiUra2018PRTEInference supports orthogonal regular PRTE asymptotics, but not the stated pathwise differentiability and square-integrable Riesz condition for the conditional quantile maps entering this partial-ID OT endpoint.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:active-face-gap / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The main limit-law and CI claims are explicitly restricted to the unique-active-face subclass; tied or slack transport faces are not claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The endpoint experiment, limit law, and fixed-alpha CI program are conditional on the active-face gap holding on the boundary-local region.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the boundary radius by a nondegenerate quantile equation, e.g. the supremum or unique solution of n t^2 V_P(t) near one, and restate the rate theorem with order bounds if only tail comparability is assumed.

Strengthen ass:boundary-tail to regular variation T_P(t) ~ c_B t^alpha if asymptotic equivalence constants are needed, or weaken prop:phase-normalization from asymptotic equivalence to two-sided order.

Split prop:strict-overlap-reduction onto a separate strict-overlap class rather than deriving it inside P_n(alpha), and state the continuity/degeneracy bridge needed to recover Imbens-Manski-Stoye calibration.

Anchor the adaptation theorem to a named Cai-Low ordered modulus or Robins-van der Vaart testing-rate result, and name the intended Le Cam two-point or fuzzy-pair construction inside the CCOT PRTE class.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the novelty kernel is genuine, but the boundary-normalization definition and tail assumption must be repaired before Stage 0.
